name: Presidio
what: PII detection and anonymization toolkit for text and structured data, with analyzers, recognizers, and anonymizers.
solves: Detecting, redacting, or anonymizing personal data in logs, text fields, support transcripts, or AI/LLM pipelines.
users: Engineering, privacy, and AI teams that need PII-aware data processing.
fit: Good tactical fit if Hylono wants privacy-aware AI/search/logging pipelines, but it is a component/toolkit, not a standalone business app.
runtime: Python services/libraries, optional REST services, recognizers, and NLP dependencies.
components: Analyzer, anonymizer, recognizers, optional image/text pipelines, and REST services via Docker or Python.
deploy: Often deployed as internal microservice or embedded library. Operational burden is moderate but narrower than full privacy platforms.
deps: Python runtime, NLP models, containers if service-based, and integration to upstream text/data flows.
datastores: None inherently; it processes data in-flight.
auth: Depends on how Hylono wraps it (internal service auth, API gateway, etc.).
apis: Python library APIs and REST service endpoints.
security: Useful for reducing privacy exposure, but should not be mistaken for complete governance or compliance tooling.
setup_path: Use as an internal service/library only if there is a concrete PII-processing need (for example AI assistants, transcript processing, support tooling, or log redaction).
env: Model/config settings, recognizer config, service port/base URL if containerized.
persistence: Stateless by default unless Hylono adds storage around it.
operations: Model quality tuning, false positive/negative review, and service versioning matter.
failure_points: Assuming out-of-the-box recognizers fully match multilingual or domain-specific data.
integration: Useful with AI pipelines, search, logs, chat/support tooling, and privacy-by-design data handling. Can sit behind Kong or be called from n8n/Next.js backends.
verify: Identify real PII-processing workflows before deploying.
risk: Low to moderate; mostly a usefulness question.
gdpr: Helpful for GDPR minimization but not a complete compliance program.
recommendation: USE AS A COMPONENT IF PII PIPELINES EXIST
overlap: Fides and CookieConsent solve different layers; Presidio is for data detection/anonymization.
codex_read_first: Read the actual PII-processing workflows before planning Presidio deployment.
codex_best_path: Expose Presidio as an internal service or utility library where text redaction is needed.
codex_never_assume: Never treat PII detection as perfectly accurate or as a substitute for governance.
